verdict_reasoning: TMED10 is a type I transmembrane protein with documented plasma membrane localization, but its canonical, dominant compartment is the ER-Golgi intermediate compartment (ERGIC) and Golgi where it functions as a cargo receptor in the secretory pathway. The lumenal domain can reach the plasma membrane, but this state is secondary to ER retention enforced by its cytoplasmic domain. The protein has a stable but minority PM pool arising from its inherent TM architecture and its role in trafficking, placing it in dual localization category—the protein body does reach the outer leaflet via its TM anchor, but this is not the dominant baseline state.
